title: Evangelion 111 Blu ray and Its Super Bit Mapping Technology
display_title: Evangelion 111 Blu ray and Its Super Bit Mapping Technology
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 1.11 Blu-ray and Its Super Bit Mapping Technology             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Apr 30, 2010 12:45 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: those screenshots showcase some fancy new technology Sony will be debuting on the 1.11 blu-ray called Super Bit Mapping for Video original sources: http://av.watch.impress.co.jp/docs/news/20090512_168327.html http://www.sony.co.jp/SonyInfo/News/Press/200905/09-0512/index.html

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 30, 2010 12:57 am Funimation 1.11 isn't using 1.11's technology or the Japanese masters, I guess? I'm assuming Japanese 1.11 doesn't have this problem. Either way, epic fail that nobody at Funi noticed this beforehand. (I'm so glad I'm holding out for a Kara boxset..)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:01 am The screenshot is from a .m2ts from the Japanese Blu-ray. The Funimation Blu-ray is actually slightly more artifacty, probably because the video was encoded/compressed once for the Japanese Blu-ray then again for Funimation's Blu-ray. The upper two sceenshots are from the page TMBH linked to and aren't much like what appears on the actual Blu-ray. Anyway, I found this informative article on SBM. Also, this one . In the near future I'll compare scenes that show clearer banding when I watch them on my computer to watching them on my HDTV via the PS3. I don't remember if the PS3 is pre-2008 or not, but I'm pretty sure it was acquired in 2009.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:11 am Now I can't blame Kara OR Funi if their BDs were outdated (so to speak) technology-wise. I do hope there is some way to rectify this problem, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:24 am I dont understand.What exactly am I looking at?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:29 am Shinji's fine ass. Duh.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:56 am Really?Meh.I've seen better.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Fri Apr 30, 2010 7:13 am Eva 04 wrote: Really?Meh.I've seen better.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Fri Apr 30, 2010 8:47 am Eva 04 wrote: I dont understand.What exactly am I looking at?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:17 pm Eva 04 wrote: I dont understand.What exactly am I looking at?

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Fri Apr 30, 2010 1:27 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The topmost image has some pretty blatant banding of colours on Shinji's ass. The other images don't.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Apr 30, 2010 2:01 pm There's nothing wrong with the BluRays. They were released with some new technology that could only be realized with special Sony BD players. Whether that new technology actually does anything, who knows? It looks like the images that they advertised with (the topmost 2) were brightened to emphasize the banding. There were several other images that were used and they all had noticeable banding. My copy of the BD is fine and I don't notice it.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri Apr 30, 2010 2:47 pm Previously discussed here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=238856 Don't know if we figured out exactly what the technology was. EDIT: Aha, it looks like NAW is doing a follow-up to the previous discussion. There just wasn't a link to the old thread for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Apr 30, 2010 4:31 pm Baz wrote: There just wasn't a link to the old thread for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by rilocay » Sat May 01, 2010 5:43 am NAveryW's own BR shot looks great, the normal and brightened version doesn't have the issue the first pic does, so is the problem on standalone blurays other than Sony brands or is it on the actual disc? Either way the actual bluray screen looks fine here. I don't own it myself yet though.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat May 01, 2010 9:49 am I don't care. At least Funimation's discs are better than Manga Entertainment's.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat May 01, 2010 2:26 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't care. At least Funimation's discs are better than Manga Entertainment's.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat May 01, 2010 8:39 pm tomrule123 wrote: Why compare when Manga Entertainment didn't... wait, Manga did release the austrailian DVD's of Rebuild, didn't they? CURSE YOU, MANGA!

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat May 01, 2010 9:58 pm Of all things to use as example..Was Shinjis ass really the right choice? Maybe Lili would like it

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Wed May 05, 2010 10:47 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't care. At least Funimation's discs are better than Manga Entertainment's.
